title: China-to-Tanzania EV Charger Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: 50 Hz Electrical Settings and China GB/T 18487 Mapping
rows.target_requirement: Tanzania charging projects should configure EVSE to local AC supply conditions and DNO connection limits, including 50 Hz operation, voltage range, phase arrangement, earthing, protection coordination, and power-quality constraints. EWURA's guidance points charging safety to IEC 61851 and requires DNO assessment for higher-load chargers; IEC 61851-1 covers EVSE operating conditions and electrical safety. China GB/T 18487 evidence should be checked against these Tanzania site settings rather than assumed transferable.
target_standards: EWURA Guidelines for E-Mobility Charging Stations and Battery Swapping 2026
DNO connection conditions for AC supply and protection settings
rows.china_equivalent: China GB/T 18487.1-2023 is current for general requirements, while GB/T 18487.5-2024 and GB/T 20234 / GB/T 27930 updates address DC systems and communication for GB/T connectors. These China standards are valuable baselines, but Tanzania acceptance depends on IEC 61851 mapping, local supply frequency and voltage settings, connector choice, DNO assessment, and site commissioning evidence.
rows.gap: Document nameplate frequency, firmware grid settings, AC input tolerance, derating at 50 Hz, RCD and protective-device selection, earthing system compatibility, and a commissioning checklist that proves the China GB/T configuration has been adapted to the Tanzania site.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory or commissioning-critical where DNO connection, IEC 61851 safety review, electrical installation inspection, or completion certificate depends on site electrical settings
mandatory_status.cn: China GB/T 18487 / GB/T 20234 compliance is a baseline, not proof that the charger is configured for Tanzania 50 Hz site conditions

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Verify 50 Hz and site electrical settings during Tanzania commissioning. A current GB/T file helps, but it does not prove local grid adaptation.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation / Standardization Administration of China

rows.target_requirement: EWURA recommends IEC 62196 Type 2 for AC charging and CCS Type 1 / CCS Type 2 for DC fast charging; it also states that CCS is incompatible with CHAdeMO and GB/T charging stations because of different communication protocols. The same guidance accepts GB/T 20234 and IEC 62196-3 (CCS2) for DC use, and says connectors for two- and three-wheelers may be used if they meet applicable technical and safety standards. Connector choice in Tanzania is therefore a project and fleet interoperability decision, not a simple China-plug export decision.
target_standards: EWURA Guidelines for E-Mobility Charging Stations and Battery Swapping 2026
rows.china_equivalent: China chargers commonly use GB/T 20234 AC or DC connectors and GB/T communication assumptions. These can be relevant for Chinese vehicles or GB/T fleets, but they may not serve IEC 62196 Type 2 or CCS vehicles without a different connector, protocol support, adapter strategy, or separate charger configuration.
rows.gap: Define the target vehicle population before shipment, then document connector type, vehicle inlet compatibility, communication protocol, adapter limits, labels, user instructions, and whether the site will provide GB/T, Type 2, CCS2, or mixed outlets.

mandatory_status.target: Project- and approval-relevant; EWURA guidance recommends or accepts named connector families, while actual mandatory configuration depends on public/private use, vehicle fleet, project approvals, and safety review
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 20234 conformity does not by itself prove IEC 62196 / CCS interoperability

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Connector mismatch is a market-access risk even when the charger is electrically safe. Map GB/T, Type 2, and CCS compatibility before site design is frozen.

source.publisher: Energy and Water Utilities Regulatory Authority (EWURA)

rows.requirement: TANESCO / DNO Grid Connection, Load, and Completion Review
rows.target_requirement: EWURA's 2026 guidance states that a person seeking to set up a public charging station may apply to the Distribution Network Operator for connectivity, and that public charging infrastructure needs a dedicated or adequately sized supply line and, where technically justified by the DNO, a dedicated transformer and protection equipment. Private captive charging may set capacity by operational need, but for chargers above 22 kW the DNO must be notified for grid-impact assessment before connection. Public and private charging infrastructure must be operational only after inspection, testing, and issuance of a completion certificate by licensed electrical personnel.
target_standards: EWURA Guidelines for E-Mobility Charging Stations and Battery Swapping 2026
DNO connection conditions and applicable grid codes
EWURA-licensed electrical installation personnel completion certificate
rows.china_equivalent: China GB/T charger documentation and Chinese site-connection drawings do not replace Tanzania DNO or TANESCO load assessment, transformer and protection decisions, metering architecture, local electrical installation sign-off, or EWURA-licensed personnel requirements.
china_standards: China low-voltage or medium-voltage site design package
rows.gap: Prepare load calculations, diversity assumptions, single-line diagrams, transformer sizing, protection coordination, earthing drawings, metering layout, harmonic or power-quality information where relevant, installer licence evidence, commissioning tests, and DNO correspondence.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where DNO connection, grid-impact assessment, licensed electrical installation, inspection, testing, or completion certificate is required; chargers above 22 kW trigger DNO notification under the EWURA guidance
mandatory_status.cn: China charger conformity does not replace Tanzania site connection, inspection, testing, or completion certification

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A charger certificate is not a grid-connection approval. Clear DNO or TANESCO load, protection, metering, and completion-certificate issues before energization.

source.publisher: Energy and Water Utilities Regulatory Authority (EWURA)

rows.target_requirement: EWURA's 2026 e-mobility charging-station guidance says EV charging safety guidance will be as per IEC 61851. IEC 61851-1:2017 covers EV supply equipment characteristics and operating conditions, the EVSE-to-EV connection, and electrical safety requirements for EVSE up to 1,000 V AC or 1,500 V DC rated supply voltage. Tanzania projects should be prepared to present IEC 61851-based safety files for public and private charging infrastructure.
target_standards: EWURA Guidelines for E-Mobility Charging Stations and Battery Swapping 2026
IEC 61851 series for electric vehicle conductive charging systems
rows.china_equivalent: China GB/T 18487 is the usual Chinese conductive-charging-system baseline and is technically useful, but a China-only GB/T report should be mapped clause-by-clause to IEC 61851 where Tanzania customers, EWURA, TBS, DNOs, or conformity assessors request IEC evidence.
rows.gap: Prepare IEC 61851 test reports or a documented GB/T-to-IEC gap matrix covering control functions, protective functions, earthing, insulation, emergency shutdown, markings, installation instructions, and rated input/output limits.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory or approval-relevant where EWURA, TBS, DNO, project owner, or conformity assessor applies the 2026 e-mobility guidelines or IEC-based technical requirements
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 18487 evidence is useful but should not be treated as automatically equivalent to IEC 61851 evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A China GB/T 18487 file is not enough by itself when Tanzania review asks for IEC 61851. Provide IEC evidence or a defensible clause mapping.

source.publisher: Energy and Water Utilities Regulatory Authority (EWURA)

rows.requirement: Tanzania EV Policy and EWURA 2026 Guidance Status
rows.target_requirement: EWURA states that Tanzania is developing policies to promote e-mobility adoption and that, pending fully functioning e-mobility policies and legislation, EWURA developed the 2026 guidelines to guide charging and battery-swapping infrastructure. The guidelines apply to individuals, companies, and institutions that aim to install, operate, and maintain public and private charging stations and battery-swapping stations in Mainland Tanzania, coordinated with authorities including TBS, NEMC, TANROADS/TARURA, LGAs, and TCRA where applicable.
target_standards: EWURA Guidelines for E-Mobility Charging Stations and Battery Swapping 2026
Future National E-Mobility Policy referenced by EWURA
rows.china_equivalent: China's GB/T charger standards and domestic EV infrastructure practice do not answer Tanzania site-policy issues such as public versus private station classification, charge-point-operator obligations, installation personnel licensing, data reporting, road or land-use approvals, environmental interfaces, or future e-mobility policy changes.
rows.gap: Classify the site as public, private, captive, or battery-swapping; identify EWURA, TBS, DNO, road, land-use, communications, and environmental touchpoints; and keep a change-control check because Tanzania's e-mobility policy framework is still developing.

mandatory_status.target: Approval-relevant under current EWURA guidance; future mandatory requirements may change as Tanzania completes e-mobility policies and legislation
mandatory_status.cn: China product standards do not address Tanzania operator, site, or policy obligations

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Tanzania's e-mobility regime is active but still developing. Treat EWURA's 2026 guidance as a live project requirement and monitor future policy or legal updates.

source.publisher: Energy and Water Utilities Regulatory Authority (EWURA)

rows.requirement: TBS Import Conformity and PVoC Scope
rows.target_requirement: TBS states that PVoC verifies goods to be imported into Tanzania against applicable national standards before shipment, and that consignments subject to PVoC must obtain mandatory customs-clearance documents and a Certificate of Conformity before shipping. EV chargers should therefore be checked by HS code, model, power rating, charger type, and shipment route against TBS regulated-goods and destination-inspection lists rather than assuming all EVSE has one identical product-certificate route.
target_standards: Tanzania Standards Act No. 2 of 2009 section 4(1)(s)
TBS Destination Inspection for regulated goods without CoC
rows.china_equivalent: China factory test reports, CCC-adjacent electrical safety files, and GB/T 18487 / GB/T 20234 evidence may support conformity review, but they do not replace TBS PVoC, CoC, destination inspection, or Tanzania customs-clearance requirements where the consignment is regulated.
rows.gap: Confirm the HS code and regulated-goods status before shipment, appoint the relevant PVoC service provider if required, and align the test-report package to the Tanzania standard or accepted IEC basis requested by the conformity assessor.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the exact consignment is subject to TBS PVoC or destination inspection; scope must be verified by HS code and current TBS lists
mandatory_status.cn: China GB/T evidence may support review but does not replace Tanzania import conformity or customs-clearance documents

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat TBS import conformity as a shipment gate. Verify PVoC or destination-inspection scope before the goods leave China.
